exact_message: HANDOFF.md is missing corrected detector text: locator-assisted, gold-validated
proximate_cause: HANDOFF.md wraps the required phrase as 'locator-assisted,\ngold-validated'. scripts/validate_detector_methodology_correction.py matched raw substrings with no whitespace normalization, so a correct sentence failed a required-phrase scan purely because of line width.

recurrence.bug_class: a markdown formatting artifact silently defeats a required-phrase scan
prior_instances: iter214 handoff seal: the same phrase 'locator-assisted, gold-validated' wrapped and failed the same scanner in the previous session
iter219 amendment A1 guard: a wrapped phrase defeated the guard's own required-phrase check during development
iter219 validate_current_paper: a blockquote continuation marker normalized to 'right > reason' and defeated a required-phrase check

root_cause.statement: Iter219's local closure ran a hand-listed subset of guards written from memory. scripts/validate_detector_methodology_correction.py was never in that list.
root_cause.why_it_matters: A verification set chosen by the same agent that wrote the change is not an independent check. It is Standard 9 at local scale: the gate passes against the author's idea of the gate rather than the real one. Reflowing the sentence would repair this instance and leave the mechanism intact.
root_cause.correction: scripts/run_ci_closure.py derives its command list from .github/workflows/ci.yml and honours the workflow's env overrides, so local closure equals CI closure and cannot drift.

unaffected: every iter219 yield, control, interval, exact paired test, and bar verdict
all 729 tests, which passed in each failed job
